rationale: Both papers center on how promotional gains (promotional credit vs unexpected in-store coupons) change spending behavior, and both report higher total/unplanned spending outcomes in field-like shopping settings. The cited paper provides a mechanism-oriented prior account (psychological income/mood effects of coupons) that could plausibly be a theory/comparison source for the citing paper’s promotion-spending effects. Given this substantive topical and outcome overlap, it is not safe to treat the citation as an obvious incidental mention.
